sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: LEFT caused a board change! The animation changed 102 cells across the full board. Let me check what changed - did the blue squares swap positions? Looking at the image, it seems like the blue squares might have swapped positions. Let me verify by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after LEFT
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"  Blue node id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("=== AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"  Blue node id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Also check black figure boundaries
print("\n=== Black figure (id=3) ===")
for n in before_seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 3:
        print(f"  Before boundary: {n['boundary']}")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 3:
        print(f"  After boundary: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The blue squares moved:
- Left blue: was at cols 19-23, now at cols 14-18 (moved left by 5)
- Right blue: was at cols 39-43, now at cols 44-48 (moved right by 5)
But the black figure boundary changed completely - it seems like the entire board shifted or the colors swapped. Wait, looking more carefully at the after boundary for id=3, it looks like the orange region's boundary! The node IDs might have shifted.
Let me look at all nodes after the change to understand what happened.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all nodes after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== ALL nodes after LEFT ===")

    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} children={n['children']}")

# Check the ascii to see what the board looks like now
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\n--- After LEFT: bottom region ---")
for i in range(45, 59):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {lines[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

1. Moved the left blue square 5 pixels to the left (from cols 19-23 to cols 14-18)

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check borders
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  White node id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
